message:
fix(ci): use $(whoami) instead of $USER for reliable owner detection

In GitHub Actions runners, the $USER environment variable is not always
set (particularly in container-based jobs), which caused the
`sudo chown $USER /dp` step to fail silently or chown to an empty
string. $(whoami) queries the OS directly and works reliably across
all runner environments.

Applied to all three platform jobs (linux-amd64, linux-arm64, macos).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -74,7 +74,7 @@ jobs:
       - name: Clone rich_rust dependency
         run: |
           sudo mkdir -p /dp
-          sudo chown $USER /dp
+          sudo chown "$(whoami)" /dp
           git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/rich_rust.git /dp/rich_rust
       - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
         with:
@@ -109,7 +109,7 @@ jobs:
       - name: Clone rich_rust dependency
         run: |
           sudo mkdir -p /dp
-          sudo chown $USER /dp
+          sudo chown "$(whoami)" /dp
           git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/rich_rust.git /dp/rich_rust
       - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
         with:
@@ -146,7 +146,7 @@ jobs:
       - name: Clone rich_rust dependency
         run: |
           sudo mkdir -p /dp
-          sudo chown $USER /dp
+          sudo chown "$(whoami)" /dp
           git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/rich_rust.git /dp/rich_rust
       - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
         with:
